refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve an operational availability equal to or greater than the customer-defined target over the agreed evaluation period.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was mostly atomic but slightly ambiguous due to the phrase 'meet or exceed' and the lack of explicit mention of the evaluation period. Refining it clarifies the requirement to focus solely on the operational availability metric relative to the customer-defined target and specifies the evaluation period, keeping it solution-neutral and focused on what the system must achieve.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the operational availability of the NGHE solution over the agreed evaluation period and compare it against the customer-defined target to verify compliance.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall achieve an operational availability equal to or greater than the customer-defined target over the agreed evaluation period.

candidate_dps: Design system with redundant critical components to ensure failover capability
Implement continuous monitoring and predictive maintenance to minimize downtime
Establish operational procedures and safety plans to reduce human error and incidents
Incorporate modular design to allow quick replacement or repair of faulty parts
Use real-time availability tracking and reporting to meet customer-defined targets
selected_dp.selected_dp: Design system with redundant critical components to ensure failover capability

potential_sources: Redundancy mechanisms may interact with monitoring systems if not properly isolated
coupling_check.notes: Selected DP focuses on redundancy to achieve availability independently; monitoring and operational procedures can be implemented as separate DPs to maintain decoupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes redundancy can be implemented without excessive cost or complexity
Risk that redundancy alone may not meet availability if operational policies are weak
Assumes evaluation period and customer targets are well-defined and stable
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including monitoring and operational procedures as separate DPs to cover all aspects influencing availability and maintain independence among design parameters.

im_boundary.name: Safety Services
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for ensuring system safety including redundancy and failover mechanisms

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends alerts when failover or redundancy issues are detected

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives and sends system health and status data relevant to safety and redundancy

module_containment_scores.notes: Very broad module, contains many functions beyond safety; redundancy logic would be diffused across many submodules.
Vehicle module includes hardware and operational aspects; redundancy is relevant but may require interfacing with other modules.
Focuses on remote control; redundancy of critical components is less central here, leading to poor containment and high diffusion.
Core responsibility includes safety and failover; best containment with minimal diffusion outside this module.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Safety Services module is the most appropriate boundary to contain the DP of designing redundant critical components for failover, as it directly manages safety-related functions and can encapsulate redundancy mechanisms with minimal coupling to other modules.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle module for hardware-level redundancy implementation details.
Interfaces with Telemetry and SafetyAlert must be well-defined to avoid leakage of redundancy logic.
If failover mechanisms require coordination with Teleoperation, cross-module dependencies may increase.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP of designing the system with redundant critical components to ensure failover capability directly addresses the FR of achieving operational availability equal to or greater than the customer-defined target. Redundancy and failover are standard and effective design parameters to meet high availability requirements, aligning well with the acceptance criteria of measured availability ≥ 0.97.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle module for hardware-level redundancy implementation details.
Interfaces with Telemetry and SafetyAlert must be well-defined to avoid leakage of redundancy logic.
If failover mechanisms require coordination with Teleoperation, cross-module dependencies may increase.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The Safety Services module has the highest containment score (9) and lowest diffusion score (2), indicating it is the most appropriate IM boundary to encapsulate the DP. It is responsible for safety and failover mechanisms, minimizing diffusion of redundancy logic across other modules. However, some diffusion risk remains due to necessary dependencies and interfaces with Vehicle, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, and possibly Teleoperation modules.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible by monitoring the Safety Services module outputs, such as SafetyAlert signals and Telemetry data, to confirm failover events and redundancy status. Operational availability can be measured over the evaluation period as per acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the FR to the Safety Services IM is clear and logical, as this module is explicitly responsible for safety and failover, directly supporting the availability requirement.

recommended_revisions: Define and document clear interface contracts between Safety Services and Vehicle, Teleoperation, Telemetry, and SafetyAlert modules to minimize diffusion and ensure encapsulation of redundancy logic.
Establish coordination protocols if failover mechanisms require cross-module interaction, especially with Teleoperation, to manage dependencies effectively.
Consider modularizing hardware-level redundancy details within the Vehicle module while maintaining control logic within Safety Services to reduce diffusion risk.
Include verification steps in the Safety Services module to monitor and report availability metrics aligned with the customer-defined target.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current level of decomposition adequately addresses the functional requirement with a suitable design parameter and implementation module. The Safety Services module is appropriately selected as the IM boundary with manageable diffusion risks and clear verification feasibility. Given the high safety criticality, further decomposition is not necessary at this stage and focus should be on implementing recommended revisions to manage diffusion risks and verification.

revise.why: To define and document clear interface contracts and coordination protocols between Safety Services and related modules to minimize diffusion and ensure encapsulation of redundancy logic, as well as to modularize hardware-level redundancy details within the Vehicle module.

assumptions: The current DP of redundant critical components sufficiently meets the availability FR.
Safety Services module can encapsulate the failover logic effectively.
Verification through Safety Services outputs is feasible and reliable.
open_risks: Dependency on Vehicle module for hardware-level redundancy details may cause diffusion risk.
Interfaces with Telemetry, SafetyAlert, and Teleoperation modules require careful management to avoid leakage of redundancy logic.
Cross-module coordination protocols need to be established to manage dependencies.
evidence_needed: Documentation of interface contracts between Safety Services and other modules.
Verification plan and results monitoring availability metrics from Safety Services outputs.
Implementation details showing modularization of hardware redundancy within Vehicle module.
